title: Rust Will Help Linux Succeed and Makes Coding Fun, Says Greg Kroah-Hartman - Slashdot
excerpt: ZDNet reports on June's Open Source Summit India 2026 in Mumbai, where Linux stable kernel maintainer Greg Kroah-Hartman gave a talk titled "Rust and Linux: How the Rust Language is Going to Help Linux Succeed." Kroah-Hartman said in his keynote that "the [Linux] kernel is moving toward Rust. G...
content_html:
ZDNet reports on June's Open Source Summit India 2026 in Mumbai, where Linux stable kernel maintainer Greg Kroah-Hartman gave a talk titled "Rust and Linux: How the Rust Language is Going to Help Linux Succeed." Kroah-Hartman said in his keynote that "the [Linux] kernel is moving toward Rust. Git is moving toward Rust. Lots of projects are starting to move toward Rust."

He didn't always feel that way. Kroah-Hartman added, "A number of years ago, when a friend of mine said, 'Ah, you got to try this new language. It's called Rust.' I was like, 'What? No, C is great.' His friend continued, "'No, no, no! It makes programming fun again.' I'm like, 'Nah, programming is fun in C.' He was right. I should have done it then. Rust is actually fun. It makes programming fun. It takes a lot of stuff away from having to worry about the compiler, which can fix a lot of your problems for you, and it makes code a little bit better."

So, Kroah-Hartman has moved from being a Rust skeptic to one of its strongest champions inside the kernel. He now regards Rust as a permanent part of Linux, not an experiment. His case is straightforward: Rust's ownership and type system can eliminate most of the "stupid little tiny things" that dominate kernel Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs), while making life easier for overworked maintainers. "Rust," in short, "makes my life so much easier...." In India, he said Linux sees "about 13 CVEs a day" and has been running at "almost nine changes an hour" for a decade or more. Most of those vulnerabilities, he argued, are not exotic attacks but simple C mistakes — unchecked pointers, forgotten unlocks, and sloppy cleanup paths: "This is what we're fixing 13 times a day. Small, trivial, little bugs like this all the time.... I've seen every CVE the kernel has done in the past 25 years. I think 80% would be gone, just because they would be caught by Rust." The remaining 20% are the logic bugs he'd prefer to focus on...."

Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems. "New drivers for some subsystems are only going to be accepted in Rust...." he said. Binder, the Android IPC mechanism at the heart of billions of devices, now has parallel C and Rust implementations in the kernel. The C version "will go away soon," leaving the Rust version "as the bedrock of all Android devices going forward."

Most of those vulnerabilities, he argued, are not exotic attacks but simple C mistakes — unchecked pointers, forgotten unlocks, and sloppy cleanup paths: "This is what we're fixing 13 times a day. Small, trivial, little bugs like this all the time.... I've seen every CVE the kernel has done in the past 25 years. I think 80% would be gone, just because they would be caught by Rust." The remaining 20% are the logic bugs he'd prefer to focus on...." Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems. It makes programming fun. It takes a lot of stuff away from having to worry about the compiler, which can fix a lot of your problems for you, and it makes code a little bit better."

sentences.zh: ZDNet 报道了 6 月份在孟买举行的 2026 年印度开源峰会，Linux 稳定内核维护者 Greg Kroah-Hartman 发表了题为“Rust 和 Linux：Rust 语言如何帮助 Linux 成功”的演讲。

sentences.zh: Kroah-Hartman 在他的主题演讲中表示，“[Linux] 内核正在向 Rust 方向发展。

sentences.en: Git is moving toward Rust.
sentences.zh: Git 正在向 Rust 迈进。

sentences.en: Lots of projects are starting to move toward Rust."
sentences.zh: 许多项目开始转向 Rust。”

sentences.zh: 他并不总是有这样的感觉。

sentences.en: It's called Rust.'
sentences.zh: 它的名字叫“铁锈”。

sentences.en: I was like, 'What?
sentences.zh: 我当时想，‘什么？

sentences.en: No, C is great.'
sentences.zh: 不，C 很棒。”

sentences.en: His friend continued, "'No, no, no!
sentences.zh: 他的朋友继续说道：“‘不，不，不！

sentences.en: It makes programming fun again.'
sentences.zh: 它让编程再次变得有趣。”

sentences.en: I'm like, 'Nah, programming is fun in C.'
sentences.zh: 我想，‘不，用 C 语言编程很有趣。’

sentences.en: He was right.
sentences.zh: 他是对的。

sentences.en: I should have done it then.
sentences.zh: 我当时就应该这么做。

sentences.en: Rust is actually fun.
sentences.zh: Rust 实际上很有趣。

sentences.en: It makes programming fun.
sentences.zh: 它让编程变得有趣。

sentences.en: It takes a lot of stuff away from having to worry about the compiler, which can fix a lot of your problems for you, and it makes code a little bit better."
sentences.zh: 它让你不必担心编译器，这可以帮助你解决很多问题，并且让代码变得更好一些。”

sentences.en: So, Kroah-Hartman has moved from being a Rust skeptic to one of its strongest champions inside the kernel.
sentences.zh: 因此，Kroah-Hartman 已经从 Rust 怀疑论者转变为内核中最强大的支持者之一。

sentences.en: He now regards Rust as a permanent part of Linux, not an experiment.
sentences.zh: 他现在将 Rust 视为 Linux 的永久组成部分，而不是一个实验。

sentences.en: His case is straightforward: Rust's ownership and type system can eliminate most of the "stupid little tiny things" that dominate kernel Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs), while making life easier for overworked maintainers.
sentences.zh: 他的例子很简单：Rust 的所有权和类型系统可以消除大多数主导内核常见漏​​洞和暴露 (CVE) 的“愚蠢的小东西”，同时让劳累过度的维护人员的生活变得更轻松。

sentences.en: "Rust," in short, "makes my life so much easier...."
sentences.zh: 简而言之，“Rust 让我的生活变得更加轻松……”

sentences.en: In India, he said Linux sees "about 13 CVEs a day" and has been running at "almost nine changes an hour" for a decade or more.
sentences.zh: 他表示，在印度，Linux“每天会看到大约 13 个 CVE”，并且十多年来一直以“每小时几乎九个更改”的速度运行。

sentences.zh: 他认为，这些漏洞中的大多数不是奇异的攻击，而是简单的 C 错误——未经检查的指针、忘记解锁和草率的清理路径：“这就是我们每天要修复 13 次的问题。

sentences.en: Small, trivial, little bugs like this all the time....
sentences.zh: 总是有这样的小、琐碎、小错误......

sentences.en: I've seen every CVE the kernel has done in the past 25 years.
sentences.zh: 我见过内核在过去 25 年中所做的每一个 CVE。

sentences.en: I think 80% would be gone, just because they would be caught by Rust."
sentences.zh: 我认为 80% 的人会消失，只是因为他们会被 Rust 抓住。”

sentences.en: The remaining 20% are the logic bugs he'd prefer to focus on...."
sentences.zh: 剩下的 20% 是他更愿意关注的逻辑错误……”

sentences.en: Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems.
sentences.zh: 此外，Rust 正在成为关键子系统中新工作的默认设置。

sentences.en: "New drivers for some subsystems are only going to be accepted in Rust...."
sentences.zh: “某些子系统的新驱动程序只会在 Rust 中被接受......”

sentences.en: he said.
sentences.zh: 他说。

sentences.en: Binder, the Android IPC mechanism at the heart of billions of devices, now has parallel C and Rust implementations in the kernel.
sentences.zh: Binder 是数十亿设备核心的 Android IPC 机制，现在在内核中具有并行的 C 和 Rust 实现。

sentences.zh: C 版本“很快就会消失”，而 Rust 版本“将成为未来所有 Android 设备的基石”。

content_html_zh: ZDNet 报道了 6 月份在孟买举行的 2026 年印度开源峰会，Linux 稳定内核维护者 Greg Kroah-Hartman 发表了题为“Rust 和 Linux：Rust 语言如何帮助 Linux 成功”的演讲。 Kroah-Hartman 在他的主题演讲中表示，“[Linux] 内核正在向 Rust 方向发展。 Git 正在向 Rust 迈进。 许多项目开始转向 Rust。” 他并不总是有这样的感觉。 克罗哈特曼补充道：“几年前，当我的一个朋友说，‘啊，你必须尝试这种新语言。 它的名字叫“铁锈”。 我当时想，‘什么？ 不，C 很棒。” 他的朋友继续说道：“‘不，不，不！ 它让编程再次变得有趣。” 我想，‘不，用 C 语言编程很有趣。’ 他是对的。 我当时就应该这么做。 Rust 实际上很有趣。 它让编程变得有趣。 它让你不必担心编译器，这可以帮助你解决很多问题，并且让代码变得更好一些。” 因此，Kroah-Hartman 已经从 Rust 怀疑论者转变为内核中最强大的支持者之一。 他现在将 Rust 视为 Linux 的永久组成部分，而不是一个实验。 他的例子很简单：Rust 的所有权和类型系统可以消除大多数主导内核常见漏​​洞和暴露 (CVE) 的“愚蠢的小东西”，同时让劳累过度的维护人员的生活变得更轻松。 简而言之，“Rust 让我的生活变得更加轻松……” 他表示，在印度，Linux“每天会看到大约 13 个 CVE”，并且十多年来一直以“每小时几乎九个更改”的速度运行。 他认为，这些漏洞中的大多数不是奇异的攻击，而是简单的 C 错误——未经检查的指针、忘记解锁和草率的清理路径：“这就是我们每天要修复 13 次的问题。 总是有这样的小、琐碎、小错误...... 我见过内核在过去 25 年中所做的每一个 CVE。 我认为 80% 的人会消失，只是因为他们会被 Rust 抓住。” 剩下的 20% 是他更愿意关注的逻辑错误……” 此外，Rust 正在成为关键子系统中新工作的默认设置。 “某些子系统的新驱动程序只会在 Rust 中被接受......” 他说。 Binder 是数十亿设备核心的 Android IPC 机制，现在在内核中具有并行的 C 和 Rust 实现。 C 版本“很快就会消失”，而 Rust 版本“将成为未来所有 Android 设备的基石”。
